rationale.R1.2: The conclusion asserts 'consistent WER reductions across read, conversational, and far-field conditions' but 4 of 6 reported conditions show the baseline at parity or better (test-other +0.1, Switchboard tie, CHiME-6 +0.5, VA-Log +0.3); the inferential leap from two favorable conditions to a universal claim is a load-bearing logical inconsistency any review should surface.
rationale.R1.3: Headline language — 'substantial gains,' 'universally applicable,' 'broadly effective recipe,' 'consistent WER reductions' — is directly contradicted by the reported numbers; evidence-claim alignment is centrally expected regardless of whether the review is primarily technical or editorial.
rationale.R1.6: A bare 'review this section' delegation always implicates prose quality; sentence-level clarity, hedge strength, and paragraph cohesion are minimally expected outputs of any review task.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical accuracy — verifying whether the reported WER numbers actually support the stated claims and conclusions — or writing quality and presentation — improving prose clarity and flow while treating the numbers as given?
